design_principles: Contexts are autonomous — no BC domain layer imports from another BC's domain layer
Coordination flows through port interfaces defined in the calling context's application layer
Adapters in the shell are the physical cross-context bridges — the only code that imports across component boundaries
Every cross-context payload is a primitive DTO — no domain object crosses a boundary
Coordination logic is embedded in the workflow graphs (TurnGraph, DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph) — no additional coordinator class is needed for a synchronous monolith
The shell composition root is the physical coordination hub — it instantiates adapters and injects them into use cases
Sagas are not needed — all bounded contexts are co-located, calls are synchronous, no distributed transaction exists

coordination_model.pattern: Sequential — each BC is called in order, blocking until the result is returned. BC-1 leads and owns the turn transaction. BC-2 is called through a port and leads its own sub-pipeline (including the BC-3 retrieval call). The TurnGraph (WF-GRAPH-1) is the de facto ConversationTurnCoordinator.
coordination_model.rationale: The RAG pipeline is inherently synchronous: evidence must be retrieved and evaluated before a response can be generated. Async coordination would add complexity without benefit. All three BCs are co-located in the same process. The port/adapter pattern already enforces context autonomy — the shell wires the adapters, and each BC sees only its own port interface.

coordinators.name: SessionInitializationCoordinator
coordinators.trigger: StartSessionCommand received from API entry point
coordinators.coordination_pattern: sequential two-context — BC-1 leads, BC-2 follows
coordinators.purpose: Coordinates session creation in BC-1 with DecisionContext initialization in BC-2. BC-1 loads all identity and history data, creates the session, then synchronously seeds BC-2 with the school anchor before the session becomes idle. Session readiness is gated on successful seeding (INV-CS-3).

coordination_flow.action: Load User, School, ConversationHistory, PurchaseHistory from repositories
coordination_flow.why: All session context must be available before Decision Intelligence can be seeded

coordination_flow.action: ConversationSession.create(user_id, school_id) + session.begin_initialization() — session enters initializing state
coordination_flow.why: Session identity must exist before it can be anchored in DecisionContext (session_id is the correlation key)
BC-2 must create the DecisionContext anchored to this school before the first turn

coordination_flow.action: DecisionContext.create(session_id, school_id, school_name) — seeds school anchor + purchase signals as initial PreferenceSignals; IDecisionContextRepository.save(context)
coordination_flow.why: Persistent context is created once per session; all future turns accumulate signals into it

coordination_flow.action: session.complete_initialization() — session transitions initializing → idle; emits SessionStarted; IConversationSessionRepository.save(session); return StartSessionResponse
coordination_flow.why: Session becomes idle (ready for turns) only after DecisionContext is confirmed initialized (enforces INV-CS-3, INV-CS-5)

invariants_upheld: INV-CS-3 — school pre-seeded into DecisionContext before session becomes active
INV-CS-5 — DecisionContext seeded at session initialization
INV-DC-2 — DecisionContext created with school anchor, not blank

coordinators.name: ConversationTurnCoordinator
coordinators.trigger: ProcessTurnCommand received from API entry point
coordinators.coordination_pattern: stateful multi-context pipeline — BC-1 classifies and orchestrates; BC-2 builds context and constructs retrieval query; BC-3 executes retrieval; BC-2 evaluates evidence; BC-1 generates, evaluates, and persists response
coordinators.purpose: Coordinates the full conversation turn workflow across all three bounded contexts. Each context performs its autonomous responsibility; the TurnGraph advances the pipeline using conditional edges driven by intent classification, context readiness, and response quality scores. This is the system's core cross-context execution engine.

coordination_flow.why: Intent determines whether retrieval is needed and which knowledge source to target

fields.note: string representation of Intent value object — not the domain object

coordination_flow.why: BC-2 needs the classified intent and raw message to extract signals and build the retrieval query from accumulated context
LLM adapter extracts structured key-value preferences from free-text message

coordination_flow.action: context.add_signal() for each extracted signal; context_snapshot updated
coordination_flow.why: Monotonic accumulation — signals are never removed, only added (INV-DC-5)

routing_outcomes: intent in _INTENT_SUFFICIENT_RETRIEVAL (referral_question) → bypass context readiness gate; proceed directly to construct_retrieval_query (message is the query; no preference signals required)
context not ready + gap detected → clarification_needed=True; skip retrieval; return to BC-1
intent is general_question / clarification_response → skip retrieval; return to BC-1 with empty evidence
context ready + retrieval intent → proceed to construct_retrieval_query
coordination_flow.why: For preference-driven intents (product_inquiry), retrieval only proceeds when context is rich enough to produce a relevant query (INV-DC-4). For intent-sufficient intents (referral_question), the question text itself is the retrieval signal — context richness is not a prerequisite and the readiness gate is bypassed to prevent silent retrieval failures on first-turn referral questions.

coordination_flow.action: RetrievalQuery.from_context() — builds enriched query string from context snapshot (signals + school anchor + intent). Vague-message backstop: if assembled content word count < _MIN_QUERY_CONTENT_WORDS (4), injects the most recent substantive prior user message from conversation_history (windowed, reversed scan) to resolve anaphorical references deterministically.
coordination_flow.why: Query is grounded in accumulated preference context, not the raw message (INV-RQ-1). History backstop prevents thin queries from anaphorical turns ('ok', 'tell me more') from producing poor recalls without requiring an extra LLM call.
BC-3 is the sole owner of all knowledge sources; only it may execute vector store retrieval

coordination_flow.action: Routes on source_target to IProductRepository or IReferralRuleRepository; executes semantic search; returns top-matching chunks

routing_outcomes: recall >= MIN_RECALL_THRESHOLD → exit subgraph, return evidence to BC-1
recall < threshold AND attempts < MAX_RETRIES → retry: back-edge to construct_retrieval_query
recall < threshold AND attempts >= MAX_RETRIES → exit with degraded evidence flagged
coordination_flow.why: Evidence quality must be evaluated before it can influence a response (INV-RE-1, INV-RE-2)

coordination_flow.action: BC-2 subgraph completes; use case saves DecisionContext; drains intra-component events (ContextUpdated, GapDetected, ContextReady); returns TurnIntelligenceResultDTO

coordination_flow.why: Response is grounded in retrieved evidence from BC-3 (relayed through BC-2); LLM never invents product or referral facts

coordination_flow.actor: TurnGraph — evaluate_response node → persist_turn or return_fallback node
coordination_flow.action: IResponseEvaluator.evaluate(response, intent, evidence) → groundedness + relevance scores
routing_outcomes: scores pass thresholds → persist_turn (history saved, turn completed)
scores fail thresholds → return_fallback (safe response, is_fallback=True)
coordination_flow.why: Quality gate before turn is committed (INV-AR-1, INV-AR-5, INV-AR-6)

coordination_state.per_turn_state: TurnGraphState (16 primitive fields) — lives in WF-GRAPH-1; is the turn-scoped coordination state for BC-1's orchestration
coordination_state.per_subgraph_state: DecisionGraphState (18 primitive fields) — lives in WF-GRAPH-2; is the turn-scoped coordination state for BC-2's sub-pipeline
coordination_state.cross_context_state_note: No domain object is shared across contexts. Context identity is correlated by session_id (a primitive string) present in all DTOs.

invariants_upheld: INV-WF-1 — intent classified before context update and retrieval
INV-WF-2 — context updated before query construction
INV-WF-3 — retrieval complete before response generation
INV-WF-4 — Decision Intelligence runs on every turn regardless of intent
INV-DC-4 — retrieval only triggered when context is partial/ready/enriched
INV-DC-5 — signals accumulate monotonically; never removed
INV-RQ-1 — retrieval query always built from context snapshot, never raw message
INV-RE-1 — evidence quality evaluated before reaches generation
INV-RE-2 — evidence with low recall triggers retry cycle
INV-AR-1 — response not delivered without passing quality thresholds
INV-AR-5 — quality failure triggers safe fallback, not a raw low-quality response
INV-AR-6 — groundedness and relevance both evaluated before turn persisted

saga_assessment.rationale: The system is a synchronous co-located monolith. All three bounded contexts run in the same process. There are no distributed transactions, no network hops between components, and no partial state that must be rolled back. The RAG pipeline is fully request-scoped: if any step fails, the exception propagates to the API layer and no cross-context state is partially committed. This is safe because: (a) BC-3 retrieval is read-only; (b) BC-2 DecisionContext is saved only after the subgraph completes (post-graph action in UC-5); (c) BC-1 ConversationSession is saved only after TurnGraph completes (post-graph action in UC-2). Each context's write is a single atomic operation at use case boundary. Introducing saga compensation would be over-engineering for this architecture.

shell_composition_role.role: Physical coordination hub. The only module in the system permitted to import across component boundaries. Instantiates cross-context adapters and injects them into use cases at startup via the dependency injection container.

adapters.name: DecisionIntelligenceAdapter
adapters.translation_responsibility: Maps PreSeedContextDTO → BC-2 use case inputs; maps TurnInputDTO → BC-2 use case inputs; maps BC-2 outputs → TurnIntelligenceResultDTO
adapters.injected_into: StartSessionUseCase (for pre_seed_context); TurnGraph (for process_turn, via ProcessTurnUseCase constructor)

adapters.name: KnowledgeRetrievalAdapter
adapters.translation_responsibility: Maps RetrievalQueryDTO → BC-3 use case input; maps BC-3 output → RetrievedEvidenceDTO

shell_composition_role.wiring: Container instantiates both adapters and injects them via constructor injection into use cases at application startup. The three BCs never import each other — they only see their own port interfaces.

folder_structure.note: Context coordination does not require a dedicated /coordination/ folder for this system. The coordination is fully materialized through the existing workflow graphs and shell adapters. No additional coordinator classes are needed.

coordination_artifacts.artifact: DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph — nested sub-coordinator for BC-2 + BC-3

coordinators.name: ConversationTurnCoordinator
coordinators.pattern: Stateful multi-context pipeline with quality gates and retry cycle

coordination_summary.sagas: None — synchronous co-located monolith, no rollback required
coordination_summary.design_verdict: Context coordination for this system is fully covered by the existing workflow orchestration (TurnGraph, DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph) combined with the shell adapters (DecisionIntelligenceAdapter, KnowledgeRetrievalAdapter). The TurnGraph IS the ConversationTurnCoordinator. No additional coordinator classes are needed. The application layer is architecturally complete.
